text: The surgeon manipulates the freed esophagus and stomach, looping a rubber band around the lower esophageal sphincter (LES). A white mesh is visible at the top, indicating a repaired hiatal hernia. The top part of the stomach is pushed behind and around the esophagus, moving it back and forth to ensure the wrap is loose enough to maintain adequate blood flow.

The surgeon sutures the stomach wrap, with the first suture connecting stomach tissue to stomach tissue. The second suture passes through the esophagus, and the final suture is placed through the lower esophageal sphincter (LES).
The surgeon completes the Nissen fundoplication, finalizing the stomach wrap around the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) to prevent reflux.
